title: Dorumaa

{{Quote|I must advise caution if you fish beyond the five-hundred-meter limit. We do have people go missing from time to time when they ignore the warnings. But that's part of the appeal for many anglers and divers who come here.|[[Rek (species)|Rek]] representative of [[Tropix Resorts]]|<ref name="Republic Commando: True Colors">''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]''</ref>}}
'''Dorumaa''' was the resort [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Almas]]. It was once an inner [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]]. Its [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] at that time crossed that of [[Oblis]]. Dorumaa was at its closest when Oblis was destroyed, and the force of that destruction thrust Dorumaa farther out in the system. It was eventually captured by the gravitation pull of Almas. Having moved far away from its suns, Dorumaa froze.<ref name="LFC">''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==History==
In [[47 BBY/Legends|47 BBY]], the [[Dorumaa Investment Group]] began terraforming Dorumaa to turn it into a resort world. Power generators were installed near the moon's core to warm it and recycle the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. Gravity generators were also installed.<ref name="LFC" />

Some of the creatures were caught when the frozen planet was thawed out. The creatures themselves were dead, but some eggs of many species managed to survive the thaw, and the seas were soon teeming with life—although the local fauna, adapting to the newer conditions, had a shorter lifespan.<ref name="LFC" /> Special attention was paid to a pair of baby [[Dorumaan leviathan|leviathans]], whose mother had apparently survived the freezing and thawing process, at least long enough to give birth to the pair.

Despite the revived water life making the moon a bit more dangerous than most resorts, Dorumaa was one of the most popular tourist destinations in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] and was terraformed to become the [[Dorumaa Resort]]. The Head Manager was young Human [[Mar Daghreb]], who controlled five property managers, each of them with five managers under their command. Dorumaa's facilities included family-oriented islands, staffed by young people so perpetually smiling that they were known as the Neo-[[Caarite]]s.<ref name="LFC" />

Unfortunately, unexpected local fauna still surprised even the investors (although sometimes, such as with the human-friendly, ridable [[Gorskin|giant sea turtles]], it was a pleasurable surprise). Local boat renter [[Rena Laut]], who considered herself an expert in local aquatic fauna, was sometimes hired as a guide to avoid "unfortunate encounters." Should any vacationers disappear, the investors solved the legal problem with a generous donation to their surviving relatives.<ref name="LFC" />

Circa 47 BBY, a research team discovered a cave, the [[Dorumaa Excavation]], with supposed writings on the wall and other evidence of an ancient, yet-unknown species living in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]]. Before any further research could be performed, the Dorumaa Investment Group built a floating land platform over the site and refused to allow access. The researchers' reports ended in [[Rorkee]], a floating city in the nearby [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Genarius]]. Rorkee built a reproduction of the dig site to attract the tourism wanting to believe in old alien species.<ref name="hitc" />

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the Cularin system joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and the first [[Senator/Legends|senator]] asked for military presence in the system to deter the frequent pirate attacks. The commanding officer of the [[Thaereian military]] forces that were sent, a [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jir Tramsig]], built his first post on Dorumaa to analyze the situation, and attacked some of the pirate bases in the [[Cularin asteroid belt|system's asteroid belt]] from the moon. Later, he built a new base on the planet [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]].<ref name="LFC" /> This did not affect the tourism industry; a week of vacation on Dorumaa was still an appreciated award, as shown when the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Alec (Jedi)|Alec]] paid one to his [[permitor]] [[Delan (permitor)|Delan]] in [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]].<ref name="cp">{{LivingForce|Clouds of Genarius: Clouded Paths}}</ref>

There was also a fishing industry in Dorumaa, including fisherman [[Drac Gerrat]]. The [[Thaereian military]] used this fact to build a base on Dorumaa: At the direction of an anonymous member, [[Identification 43641|Identification #43641]], which was suspected to be the Thaereians, the Dorumaa Investment Group built a deep-sea landing platform, officially intended as a drop-off point for deep-sea fishing.<ref name="LFC" /> Identification #43641 also announced that the evidence of native intelligent life in Dorumaa Excavation was false; however, access to the site was still forbidden.<ref name="hitc" />

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Thaereians were driven out of Dorumaa in the [[Battle of Dorumaa]].<ref name="DecisionCularin" />

After the Cularin system reappeared from its near-decade-long disappearance, [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] scientist [[Ko Sai/Legends|Ko Sai]] hid on Dorumaa from the various organizations pursuing her. She was found in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]] by [[Kal Skirata]] and his allies.
